summary: Implemented M4 campaign-only route and transport capacity planning forecasts. The new sim.preview-m4-route-transport-capacity query validates campaign references, reuses accepted M2 route/season transport preview logic, combines current M4 grain reservations and mobilized force commitments, and returns route segments, per-source bottleneck capacity, travel window, carried supply limit, overload reason, and monsoon/season risk reason codes. Scope intentionally excludes march execution, route mutation, battle, siege, UI, economy expansion, naval warfare, save-format work, server, Rust/WASM, and new dependencies.

decisions: Added one narrow protocol query kind, sim.preview-m4-route-transport-capacity, instead of adding commands or persistent planning state. This is an R3 protocol/query surface change and routes to systems_architect.
Added tests/sim-core-m4-route-transport-capacity.test.ts to the explicit @monsoon/sim-core test script so the required package test gate covers the new forecast invariants.

Forecasts are read-only and derive from existing M2 transport routes/seasonal curves plus M4 mobilizedForceCommitments and grainSupplyReservations. No WorldState mutation, route state mutation, march execution, battle, siege, UI, save-format, or economy expansion was added.

Water/river behavior remains only the simple accepted M2 routeKind capacity/cost modifier; the M4 query copies M2 preview segment data and does not introduce fleets, naval battles, amphibious logic, or independent water-route state.
Equivalent route choices rely on the existing M2 stable tie-breakers, and M4 tests lock behavior across shuffled route and reservation ordering.
Season risk reasons are deterministic reason codes derived from the planned departure month and M2 seasonal curve values on returned route segments.
risks: R3: protocol query surface changed. systems_architect should verify the query shape is acceptable before lead integration; QA review may still be required by the R3 policy before acceptance.

Aggregate carriedSupplyLimit sums per-source limits and aggregate bottleneckCapacity reports the minimum per-source bottleneck. This is intentionally a planning forecast, not reservation of shared edge capacity across simultaneous convoys.
Forecasts currently require district campaign targets. Polity-target route planning remains outside this task until a future target-to-district policy is approved.
requested_action: Review M4-ROUTE-TRANSPORT-CAPACITY-001 on branch feat/m4-route-transport-capacity. Verify the protocol query, deterministic M2 route reuse, read-only forecast behavior, overload/season reason codes, and scope boundaries. Return ACCEPT, REQUEST_CHANGES, or BLOCK; do not mark ACCEPTED or CLOSED from this writer handoff alone.
